prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `8abad3cf`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new task package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8abad3cf/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8abad3cf/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8abad3cf/generator.py#L53), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8abad3cf/notes.md#L1). The rule is frequency-based: ignore background `7`, sort the remaining colors by count, render the largest-count color as a solid `n x n` block on the right, render the next color as a lower `m x m` block in the left `7`-panel, and place the optional singleton at the bottom-left of that left panel. No new shared primitive was needed.

I discarded the Sonnet hint and corrected the Opus hint in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8abad3cf/notes.md#L1), because the singleton placement in the official examples is not at the top-left of the embedded square. The generator stays close to the task family by sampling from the three observed official input regimes and varying colors, flips/rotations, and small edge-preserving paddings.

- Generated examples: `pass 8abad3cf: 1000/1000 generated examples passed`
